meta.summary: ByteDance Seed surfaced the strongest concrete artifact with Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena alongside its new Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, signaling continued competition in multimodal and voice-native foundation models. Zhipu AI expanded its Intel partnership to ship on-device Zhipu Qingyan and CodeGeeX for AI PCs, while NVIDIA positioned Dynamo as a new inference stack for agentic coding workloads where repeated API calls and KV-cache inefficiency drive cost.

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance launches Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks a major release in the company's foundation-model lineup
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is signaling product momentum in both frontier model evaluation and real-time voice interaction, two areas with direct implications for consumer AI assistants and multimodal platform competition.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed announced Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and made it available on Arena, giving the market a visible early checkpoint for model quality and competitive positioning.
The company also introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, emphasizing attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking rather than standard push-to-talk voice UX.
The cluster included a separate 2027 foundation model campus recruiting announcement, suggesting ByteDance is pairing product rollout with continued investment in core model talent.

evidence.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Open
Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena

featured_topics.headline: Zhipu AI and Intel expand partnership for on-device Zhipu Qingyan and CodeGeeX
featured_topics.summary_short: Zhipu AI announced an expanded partnership with Intel at the Intel AI PC ecosystem event, delivering on-device versions of Zhipu Qingyan and the CodeGeeX AI coding assistant. The collaboration focuses on AI PC innovation through Zhipu's hybrid cloud-edge model product matrix
featured_topics.why_it_matters: This partnership is a concrete signal that Chinese model providers and chip vendors are pushing AI PC distribution, which matters for edge deployment strategy and enterprise endpoint AI adoption.
key_takeaways: Zhipu AI said the expanded Intel collaboration will deliver on-device versions of Zhipu Qingyan and the CodeGeeX coding assistant at Intel's AI PC ecosystem event.
The partnership centers on a hybrid cloud-edge product matrix, indicating Zhipu is not relying solely on cloud inference and is targeting local deployment scenarios.
CodeGeeX gives the announcement a developer-product angle, while Qingyan broadens it to general assistant use cases on AI PCs.

evidence.title: Zhipu AI and Intel deepen AI ecosystem partnership

featured_topics.headline: NVIDIA introduces Dynamo for agentic coding inference workloads
featured_topics.summary_short: Traditional inference wasn’t built for agentic coding. Agentic tools make hundreds of API calls per coding session, often with recomputed context, creating bottlenecks that drive up cost per token. NVIDIA Dynamo rebuilds the stack for agents with: → KV-aware routing →
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If agentic coding becomes a major inference category, infrastructure optimized for KV-cache reuse and high-call-volume sessions could materially change serving economics and platform selection.
key_takeaways: NVIDIA framed agentic coding as a distinct workload where a single session can trigger hundreds of API calls, making traditional inference stacks inefficient.
Dynamo is positioned around KV-aware routing, directly targeting wasted recomputation and context-handling bottlenecks that increase cost per token.
The announcement matters less as a model launch than as a stack-level optimization play for coding agents, where infrastructure advantage can become a product moat.

evidence.title: Traditional inference wasn’t built for agentic coding
